accurate.why: All claims are supported by the bill text; the 1865 treaty description and court case details are accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated content; all specific references to tracts, court cases, and treaty terms match the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the purpose is transferring federal lands into trust for the Winnebago Tribe.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Explains that lands are declared part of the reservation and held in trust per 1865 treaty terms.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the gaming prohibition and the 'subject to all valid existing rights' condition.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers all three tracts, the trust status, reservation declaration, and gaming prohibition.

concise.why: The detailed recitation of court case names and legal descriptions adds length beyond what is needed for a functional summary.

correct_entities.why: Winnebago Tribe of Nebraska, Indian Gaming Regulatory Act, and treaty reference are correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: Treaty year (1865), approval date (July 12, 2024), and statutory citation (25 U.S.C. 2701) are all accurate.
